#### FAIL #1 — BATS slot 62 (G2) — `DelegationManager` constructor rejects no-arg construction

  at new DelegationManager (file:///.../dist/coordination/delegation/manager.js:27:19)
```

- **Test site:** `tests/scripts/tmux/62-pool-status-api.bats:30` — `const instance = new Manager();`
- **Source of throw:** `src/coordination/delegation/manager.ts:78-89` — constructor requires either `client` arg OR `options.coordinator && options.lifecycle`; otherwise throws.
- **Contract gap:** PLAN-02 T1 implemented `getPoolSnapshot()` and `__getDelegationsForTesting` (line 484) but did not make the constructor no-arg friendly. The BATS test relies on `new Manager()` working with no args.

#### FAIL #2 — BATS slot 64 (G4) — `tmux-copilot` reports `tmux-not-wired`

```
not ok 1 tmux-copilot forward-prompt delivers sentinel-prepended text to live pane (G4)
# (in test file tests/scripts/tmux/64-forward-prompt.bats, line 47)
#   `[ "$status" -eq 0 ]' failed
{ "available": false, "reason": "tmux-not-wired" }
```

- **Test site:** `tests/scripts/tmux/64-forward-prompt.bats:42-46` — invokes `tmuxCopilotTool.execute({ action: 'forward-prompt', ... })`
- **Tool returns `{available:false, reason:"tmux-not-wired"}`:** the tool is constructed via the BATS `tmux_node_eval` runtime, but no `TmuxMultiplexer` instance is wired into the tool at construction time. The tool's `tmuxMultiplexer` field is `undefined`, so the action dispatches to the not-wired branch.

#### FAIL #3 — BATS slot 65 (G5) — same `tmux-not-wired` cause

```
not ok 1 tmux-copilot take-over / release suppresses and restores forward-prompt (G5)

```

- Same root cause as slot 64: `tmuxCopilotTool` instantiated without `TmuxMultiplexer` injection.
- **Test site:** `tests/scripts/tmux/65-takeover-release.bats:64-67`

#### FAIL #4 — BATS slot 66 (G6) — `recordDelegationTerminal` not exported

```

    at file:///Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/[eval1]:11:5
```

- **Test site:** `tests/scripts/tmux/66-session-tracker-delegation-events.bats:36-37` — destructures `recordDelegationTerminal` from `dist/features/session-tracker/tool-delegation.js`.
- **Source:** `src/features/session-tracker/tool-delegation.ts:432-447` — `recordDelegationTerminal` is a **method on the `ToolDelegation` class** (line 69), not a top-level export. Only `getDelegationEventLog` (line 35) and `clearDelegationEventLog` (line 40) are exported at module top level.
- **Contract gap:** PLAN-04 T1/T2 added the method internally + wired it from `abortDelegation`/`terminalFallback` (`manager.ts` wired paths), but the BATS test imports it as a module-level function. Either the test must be rewritten to call `new ToolDelegation().recordDelegationTerminal(...)`, or `recordDelegationTerminal` must be re-exported as a top-level helper that delegates to a shared module-level `appendDelegationEvent`.

#### FAIL #5 — BATS slot 67 (G1 grep-guard) — file does not exist

Per `58-PLAN-05.md:118`: "If slot 61 collides with P56, the implementer should rename the P58 G1 scenario to slot 67". Current disk: `tests/scripts/tmux/61-stress-test-real-world-workflow.bats` (P56, 14454 bytes) — no `67-*.bats` exists. Collision resolution was never executed.

| `npm test` (vitest) | PASS 3,310 / skip 7 / fail 0 | exceeds 3,203+ target |
